title: Artists At Work
description: A portrait series photographing artists at work — three studies in light, stillness, and the moment a performance turns inward.

blocks.text: A portrait series photographing artists at work — three studies in light, stillness, and the moment a performance turns inward.

Each frame was built around a single artist mid-process — the tools of their craft left in frame, the pose unposed. Rather than chase a polished finish, the series leans into the in-between: chalk-dusted hands, a held breath, the beat before the next brushstroke. Lighting stays low and directional throughout, letting shadow do as much storytelling as the subject.

title: LunaFit Fresh
description: Brand and campaign design for LunaFit Fresh, carried from logo system through print and in-store display.

blocks.text: Brand and campaign design for LunaFit Fresh, carried from the logo system through print and in-store display.

The brief called for something that could live comfortably on a shelf and on a phone screen — a fresh, juice-bar energy that still reads as considered rather than loud. The palette and mark were built to scale down to a bottle cap and up to a full window display without losing character, and the campaign system carries that same logic across packaging, signage, and social.

title: Sticker Portraits
description: Send your picture, and you will get a personalized portrait in the form of a sticker.

blocks.text: Send your picture, and you will get a personalized portrait in the form of a sticker.
The project was a way to sharpen my illustration and produce personalized artwork clients could reuse — as stickers, phone wallpapers, or t-shirt designs.

Love! 
Live! 
Love!

Every portrait starts the same way: a photo sent in by a stranger, then rebuilt by hand into something warmer and a little more themselves. The style stays loose and bright on purpose — thick outlines, flat color, a wink of expression — so the final sticker feels less like a copy of the photo and more like how the person already sees themselves. It's an ongoing series, one submission at a time.

title: Beautiful Web Spring Wear
description: What if spiders ran a clothing store? Beautiful Webs is a women's boutique specializing in spring dresses made from soft, vibrant silks — the shopping experience imagined as finding a field of flowers inside a forest.

blocks.text: What if spiders ran a clothing store?

Beautiful Webs is a women's boutique specializing in spring dresses made from soft, vibrant silks. The concept frames the shopping experience as discovering a field of flowers inside a forest. Brand identity, logo, and visual system built in Adobe Illustrator and Adobe Dimension.

The web motif runs through every touchpoint without tipping into costume — a delicate lattice pattern on hangtags, a spun-silk texture in the logo mark, packaging that catches light the way a web does at dawn. It's a brand built to feel a little magical without losing the softness that spring wear is supposed to have.

title: Butterfly Garden Candy Shop
description: What if butterflies ran a candy store? Many sweet treats are shaped like flowers, since this is a store run by butterflies and they love the nectar from flowers.

blocks.text: What if butterflies ran a candy store?

The treats are shaped like flowers — butterflies love floral nectar — and the confections use natural ingredients from pesticide-free farms, safe for butterflies and customers alike. The logo balances elegance with play: "Butterfly" in an elegant script, "garden" and "candy shop" grounded in a legible, approachable font.

Packaging leans into soft pastels and rounded forms so the shop feels inviting to kids and nostalgic to adults, while the butterfly mascot recurs across signage, bags, and the candy wrappers themselves — always mid-flight, always a little curious. The goal was a brand that could sell sincerity and whimsy in the same breath.

title: Castillo Sampaguita
description: A casual restaurant for families, serving cuisine brought to the Philippines and back to Valencia — built around the feeling of being transported to the islands.

blocks.text: Castillo Sampaguita is a family Spanish-Philippine restaurant that promises to transport you back to the Philippines through its cuisine and its environment.

The identity draws on bringing Philippine nature to Valencia — capturing the flora and fauna, and the connection between Pampanga, Philippines and Valencia, Spain. Authentic island spices, a child-friendly menu with visual aids, Philippine staff, and artisanal décor all work to make diners feel at home on the islands.

The mark takes its name from the sampaguita, the Philippine national flower, and carries its shape through the menu, signage, and interior details — a quiet through-line tying the space back to home even for guests who've never set foot on the islands. Warm woods, hand-lettered specials, and a muted tropical palette round out a space designed to feel lived-in rather than themed.

title: Social Cereal
description: Cereal Killer's is a cereal brand focused on mysterious and horror related themes — this time leaning cute, while keeping some darkness to it.

blocks.text: Cereal Killer's is a cereal brand built on mystery and horror. This time we try something new — leaning cute, while keeping some of the darkness.
So I bring you Annette Sugar Webs and Sinnamon Blood Drops: two mascots and two boxes that hold whimsy and dread in the same spoonful.

Every box, mascot, and social post walks the same tightrope — cute enough to shelve next to a kids' cereal, unsettling enough to reward a second look. Annette's cobwebbed pigtails and Sinnamon's drip-red frosting are played completely straight, letting the horror-cute tension do the work instead of a wink to camera.

title: House Ghosts
description: A children's book telling the tale of Amelia, a curious girl who discovers mischievous yet protective ancestral spirits guarding her family's home.

blocks.text: House Ghosts is a children's book about Amelia, a curious girl who discovers the mischievous yet protective ancestral spirits guarding her family's home.

When her family is away or asleep, the house ghosts keep the home safe from intruders. Amelia sets out to unravel her family's history, travelling through time with the spirited ancestors — facing challenges, solving puzzles, and confronting a dark force that threatens the house's legacy. A heartwarming adventure about family, heritage, and the enduring power of love and protection.

Illustrations were built to feel like storybook pages rather than static spreads — warm, textured linework and a palette that shifts with the mood of each scene, brightening for the ghosts at play and dimming for the moments of real danger. The character designs for Amelia and her ancestral guardians carry that same balance of comfort and mystery throughout.

title: Dance Perfumes
description: Two dance-inspired fragrances: “Flamenco Dancing Gypsy” and “Bolero Sensuous Step.”

blocks.text: Two dance-inspired fragrances, each translating a dance into an olfactory arc of top, heart, and base notes.

Flamenco Dancing Gypsy captures the passion and elegance of Flamenco — vibrant top notes for swift footwork, deep heart notes for the emotional storytelling, fiery base notes for the finale. Bolero Sensuous Step, inspired by the intimate Bolero duet, choreographs tender top notes, emotional heart notes, and lingering base notes into an enduring romantic bond.

Both bottles were designed as a matched pair rather than standalone objects — same silhouette, opposite temperaments, so they read as a set on a vanity or in a gift box. Flamenco's bottle takes on warm reds and gold accents to match its fire; Bolero settles into deeper plums and soft metallics built for its slower, more intimate register.

title: Jessica Jung Author Website
description: A website design for author Jessica Jung.

blocks.text: A website design for author Jessica Jung — a home for her books, her voice, and her readers.

The layout was built around the books themselves — cover art given room to breathe, a clean editorial rhythm for excerpts and reviews, and a simple path from any page back to where readers can buy or borrow. Typography stays quiet and legible throughout, letting Jessica's writing carry the site rather than competing with it.

title: XS Energy Drinks
description: An advertising and social campaign for XS Energy Drinks, spanning ads and social posts.

blocks.text: An advertising and social campaign for XS Energy Drinks — a set of ads and social posts built to move fast and feel loud.
Designed across Adobe InDesign, XD, and Keynote.

Each post and ad was built around a single high-contrast beat — bold type, saturated color, a jolt of motion implied even in a still frame — to match the jittery, electric energy the drink is selling. The system had to hold up across formats, from a single Instagram tile to a full print ad, without losing that same shot-of-energy feel.

title: Couture Queens L.A. Fashion Festival
description: Branding, website and app design for the Couture Queens L.A. Fashion Festival.

blocks.text: Branding, website, and app design for the Couture Queens L.A. Fashion Festival — one identity carried from logo to landing page to app.

The festival needed a brand that could hold its own next to the fashion it was celebrating — bold, editorial, a little glamorous — while still functioning as a practical tool for attendees. The website and companion app share one visual language for schedules, designer showcases, and ticketing, so the glamour of the branding never gets in the way of actually finding your seat.
